  # Stripe paywall (monthly subscription)                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  monetization, sprint-1  👀 You are watching            
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Introduce a monthly subscription paywall via Stripe Checkout. Test mode first. Decomposed into three child tasks:   
  Stripe account and pricing setup (KAN-41), backend webhook and entitlement guard (KAN-42), and frontend paywall     
  screen                                                                                                              
  with Checkout redirect (KAN-43).h2. Context                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  Without a monetization layer the app cannot sustain itself. This epic gates the core styling features behind a      
  subscription so the team can start measuring willingness-to-pay before scaling user acquisition.                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Stripe test-mode account, Product, and Price created and documented in backend/.env.example (KAN-41)              
  • Backend webhook flips is_subscribed on checkout events; gated endpoints return 402 for unsubscribed users (KAN-42)
  • Frontend paywall screen routes users to Stripe Checkout and correctly handles success and cancel paths (KAN-43)   
  • Full end-to-end flow verified in Stripe test mode

⭐ Task  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 20 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-42  💭 0 comments  🧵 2 linked                          
                                                                                                                      
  # BE: Stripe webhook + entitlement check on protected endpoints                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  backend, monetization, sprint-1  👀 You are watching   
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Add Stripe integration to the backend. New module: backend/src/stripe/ (controller, service, module). Requires env  
  vars from KAN-41 ( STRIPE_PRICE_ID , STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET ) — add both to backend/.env.example .                   
                                                                                                                      
  **Three deliverables:**                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  1. POST /stripe/checkout-session — creates and returns a Stripe Checkout Session URL for the subscription Price ID (
  STRIPE_PRICE_ID ).                                                                                                  
  2. POST /stripe/webhook — verifies the Stripe-Signature header using STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET , then: sets User.       
  is_subscribed = true on checkout.session.completed ; sets User.is_subscribed = false on customer.subscription.      
  deleted .                                                                                                           
  3. SubscriptionGuard — checks User.is_subscribed ; rejects with HTTP 402 on unsubscribed users; applied to the      
  protected endpoints (see open question).                                                                            
  **Schema change** : add is_subscribed Boolean @default(false) to the User model in backend/prisma/schema.prisma and 
  generate a migration.                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Webhook handler is unit-tested: valid signature + checkout.session.completed → is_subscribed flipped to true ;    
  invalid signature → 400 returned                                                                                    
  • SubscriptionGuard unit-tested: rejects a request from a user with is_subscribed = false ; allows a request with   
  is_subscribed = true                                                                                                
  • npm run test passes                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ## Needs human input                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Which endpoints should the SubscriptionGuard gate? (e.g. POST /style-pieces , POST /outfit-avatar , POST /find- 
  pieces — or a different subset?) The paywall strategy determines this.                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  BLOCKS                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-43 FE: Paywall screen + Stripe Checkout redirect       • Task • Medium • Idea                                   
                                                                                                                      
  IS BLOCKED BY                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-41 Stripe account, products, pricing setup (test mode) • Task • Medium • Idea

  # FE: Paywall screen + Stripe Checkout redirect                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  frontend, monetization, sprint-1  👀 You are watching  
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  When a logged-in user hits a gated endpoint and receives an HTTP 402, route them to a Paywall screen that:# Calls   
  POST /stripe/checkout-session (from KAN-42) to obtain a Checkout Session URL.                                       
                                                                                                                      
  1. Redirects the browser to that URL (Stripe-hosted checkout).                                                      
  2. On Stripe's success callback URL: calls getMe() from frontend/src/api.js to refresh subscription status, then    
  routes the user back into the interrupted flow.                                                                     
  3. On Stripe's cancel callback URL: returns the user to the Paywall screen without a JS error.                      
  New file: frontend/src/screens/Paywall/Paywall.jsx .                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • End-to-end test in Stripe test mode (card 4242 4242 4242 4242) completes checkout successfully and the previously 
  gated action is now allowed                                                                                         
  • Cancel path returns to the Paywall screen with no console errors                                                  
  • npm run build (in frontend/ ) passes                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  BLOCKS                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-53 A/B test paywall variants via PostHog feature flags           • Task • Medium • Idea                         
                                                                                                                      
  IS BLOCKED BY                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-41 Stripe account, products, pricing setup (test mode)           • Task • Medium • Idea KAN-42 BE: Stripe       
  webhook                                                                                                             
  + entitlement check on protected endpoints • Task • Medium • Idea

  # Results: horizontal swipe (one avatar, product carousel, descriptions visible)                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  frontend, sprint-1  👀 You are watching                
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Restructure the Results view in frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.jsx from a vertical list to a Tinder-style   
  horizontal swipe layout.                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  Changes to the ResultCard component and its parent in Discover.jsx :                                                
                                                                                                                      
  • One outfit (avatar image + item list) fills the viewport at a time; users swipe/drag horizontally to navigate     
  between outfits.                                                                                                    
  • Within each outfit card, the product list is also horizontally swipeable (one product visible per swipe).         
  • The DB-stored product description ( it.description ) is always visible on the product card — no tap-to-expand     
  required.                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Only one outfit is visible at a time; swiping left/right transitions to the adjacent outfit                       
  • Within an outfit, products are horizontally swipeable and their descriptions are always visible                   
  • Swipe gestures work on both touch (mobile) and mouse drag (desktop)                                               
  • Saving an outfit to the wardrobe still works correctly                                                            
  • npm run build (in frontend/ ) passes

⭐ Story  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 20 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-45  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                         
                                                                                                                      
  # VibeExtraction: auto-fetch celebrity photo from name                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  frontend, sprint-1  👀 You are watching                
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  After the user types a celebrity name in the Discover flow, debounce a fetch for a representative photo and display 
  it next to the extracted VibeProfile. The celebrity name input lives in frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.jsx .
                                                                                                                      
  The photo can be fetched client-side via the free Wikimedia Commons API (no key required) or proxied through the    
  backend. The chosen source must be documented in a code comment.                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Photo appears within 2 s for a well-known celebrity name after the user stops typing (debounce ≤ 500 ms after     
  input blur or typing pause)                                                                                         
  • If no photo is found, the image slot hides gracefully — no broken-image icon                                      
  • The photo source (API URL or service name) is documented in a comment in the component file                       
  • If the chosen source requires an API key, it is added to backend/.env.example or frontend/.env.example as         
  appropriate

  ⭐ Story  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 20 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-47  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                         
                                                                                                                      
  # Onboarding: brand picker grouped by category                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  frontend, sprint-1  👀 You are watching                
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Update frontend/src/screens/Onboarding/BrandSelection.jsx so brands are rendered under fashion-category headers (e.g.
  Streetwear, Y2K, Old money, Minimal) instead of a flat alphabetical list.                                           
                                                                                                                      
  The brand list comes from GET /brands (backed by backend/src/brands/brands.service.ts which reads distinct brands   
  from catalog_items ). Category membership must be defined in a new static config file — e.g.                        
  frontend/src/screens/Onboarding/brand-categories.js — since the catalog DB has no fashion-category column on brands.
                                                                                                                      
  User selections still persist via updateMe({ preferred_brands: [...] }) to User.preferred_brands (already a String[]
  column on the users table).                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Brands are rendered under labelled category headers; users can select/deselect any brand across all categories    
  • Selections save correctly via updateMe and are returned in subsequent GET /me responses                           
  • Category membership is defined in a clearly named static config file, not inline in JSX

  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  backend, sprint-1  👀 You are watching                 
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Add a POST /style-pieces/regenerate endpoint to the existing style-pieces module.                                   
                                                                                                                      
  Files to edit:                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  • backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.dto.ts — add RegenerateOutfitDto with fields: prior_outfit (the existing    
  outfit object), feedback (free-text string, e.g. "less formal", "swap shoes for sneakers"), plus the same vibe ,    
  pieces , and gender fields as StylePiecesDto to allow the model to re-query if needed.                              
  • backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.controller.ts — add @Post('regenerate') route.                              
  • backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts — add regenerate() method that injects the prior outfit and      
  feedback into the Sonnet prompt, then applies the same wearable-core validation as compose() (top+bottom or dress   
  required).                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Unit test: the feedback text appears in the prompt sent to Sonnet (assert via prompt string inspection or spy)    
  • Unit test: an outfit missing a wearable core is dropped from the response (same behaviour as POST /style-pieces ) 
  • The endpoint response shape is identical to POST /style-pieces

  # Analytics: PostHog instrumentation (FE + BE)                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  analytics, sprint-1  👀 You are watching               
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Add PostHog analytics instrumentation to the frontend and backend.                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  **Frontend** (initialise PostHog on mount in frontend/src/App.jsx ; fire events in the relevant screen components): 
                                                                                                                      
  • signup_completed — after successful registration; include {{{ gender, nationality }}} from the signup form        
  • vibe_extracted — after extractVibe() resolves in frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.jsx                       
  • outfit_generated — after stylePieces() resolves in Discover.jsx                                                   
  • buy_link_clicked — when a product buy link is tapped in Discover.jsx                                              
                                                                                                                      
  **Backend** (NestJS interceptor or middleware in backend/src/ ):                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  • api_call — every request with {{{ endpoint, status_code, duration_ms }}}                                          
                                                                                                                      
  **PII rule** : identify users by sha256(user.id) — never send the raw UUID or email address to PostHog.             
                                                                                                                      
  Note: paywall funnel events ( checkout_started , subscription_activated ) will be added as part of KAN-43 once the  
  paywall ships.                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  **Docs** : update backend/README.md with the POSTHOG_API_KEY env var and a short event catalogue.                   
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • All four frontend events appear in the PostHog live event stream after a full test run through the app            
  • The backend interceptor emits api_call with correct endpoint , status_code , and duration_ms fields               
  • POSTHOG_API_KEY is added to backend/.env.example and frontend/.env.example                                        
  • No raw UUIDs or email addresses appear in PostHog event properties                                                
  • backend/README.md lists the event catalogue                                                                       
  • npm run test passes                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  BLOCKS                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-53 A/B test paywall variants via PostHog feature flags • Task • Medium • Idea

  ⭐ Story  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 20 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-52  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                         
                                                                                                                      
  # 'Did you buy?' in-app follow-up survey                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  analytics, sprint-1  👀 You are watching               
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Show a one-time in-app survey banner after a user clicks a product buy link and later returns focus to the app.     
                                                                                                                      
  **Trigger** : listen for document.visibilitychange (state → 'visible' ); if the user has at least one un-surveyed   
  buy-                                                                                                                
  link click (tracked in component state or localStorage keyed by item ID), display the banner for that item.         
                                                                                                                      
  **Banner contents** : "Did you buy [item name]?" + Yes / No buttons + optional free-text reason field. Dismissing   
  without answering counts as a non-response and should not re-show.                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  **Frontend** : frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.jsx — within the ItemRow component or as an overlay managed by
  Discover.jsx .                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  **Backend** : new survey/ module — backend/src/survey/survey.controller.ts , survey.service.ts , survey.module.ts . 
  Endpoint: POST /survey/purchase-intent with body {{{ item_id: string, bought: boolean, reason?: string }}}.         
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Banner appears once per buy-link click when the user returns to the tab; does not re-appear for the same item on  
  subsequent returns                                                                                                  
  • Dismissing the banner (without answering) suppresses re-showing for that item                                     
  • POST /survey/purchase-intent persists the response and returns 201                                                
  • npm run test passes for the new survey service                                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Needs human input                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Should purchase_survey_responses be linked to the authenticated User (richer analysis, requires user to be      
  logged in on return) or stored anonymously with a session token? Please confirm the approach before building the    
  schema migration.                                                                                                   
  [ ] Confirm or adjust the suggested Prisma model fields: id (uuid) , user_id (uuid, nullable) , item_id (string) ,  
  bought (boolean) , reason (string, nullable) , created_at (datetime) .                                              
                                                                                                                      
[0;90m[0m[0;90m[0m  [0;90mView this issue on Jira: https://jaeyoonsworkspace-23358853.atlassian.net/browse/KAN-52[0m

  ⭐ Task  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 20 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-53  💭 0 comments  🧵 2 linked                          
                                                                                                                      
  # A/B test paywall variants via PostHog feature flags                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  analytics, backlog  👀 You are watching                
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Using PostHog feature flags, set up a 2–3 variant A/B test over the paywall screen. Each variant differs in headline
  copy and/or displayed price. Variants are assigned client-side via a PostHog flag evaluated on paywall load. Track  
  paywall_viewed , checkout_started , and subscription_completed events with a variant property on each.              
                                                                                                                      
  Depends on: Stripe paywall live (KAN-43), PostHog instrumentation live (KAN-51).                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  Provides data-driven optimisation of paywall conversion after the initial paywall and analytics are live. Even a    
  small lift in conversion rate compounds significantly with user acquisition scale.                                  
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • PostHog feature flag created with 2–3 named variants (e.g. control , price-anchor , social-proof )                
  • Paywall screen reads the active variant from PostHog and renders the corresponding copy and/or price display      
  • paywall_viewed event sent with {{{ variant }}} property on every paywall load                                     
  • PostHog experiment or funnel dashboard shows conversion rate per variant                                          
  • If the PostHog flag call fails or times out, the screen falls back to the default variant without error           
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  IS BLOCKED BY

  instrumentation                                                                                                     
  (FE + BE)  • Task • Medium • Idea

  ⭐ Task  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 20 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-53  💭 0 comments  🧵 2 linked                          
                                                                                                                      
  # A/B test paywall variants via PostHog feature flags                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  analytics, backlog  👀 You are watching                
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Using PostHog feature flags, set up a 2–3 variant A/B test over the paywall screen. Each variant differs in headline
  copy and/or displayed price. Variants are assigned client-side via a PostHog flag evaluated on paywall load. Track  
  paywall_viewed , checkout_started , and subscription_completed events with a variant property on each.              
                                                                                                                      
  Depends on: Stripe paywall live (KAN-43), PostHog instrumentation live (KAN-51).                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  Provides data-driven optimisation of paywall conversion after the initial paywall and analytics are live. Even a    
  small lift in conversion rate compounds significantly with user acquisition scale.                                  
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • PostHog feature flag created with 2–3 named variants (e.g. control , price-anchor , social-proof )                
  • Paywall screen reads the active variant from PostHog and renders the corresponding copy and/or price display      
  • paywall_viewed event sent with {{{ variant }}} property on every paywall load                                     
  • PostHog experiment or funnel dashboard shows conversion rate per variant                                          
  • If the PostHog flag call fails or times out, the screen falls back to the default variant without error           
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  IS BLOCKED BY                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-43 FE: Paywall screen + Stripe Checkout redirect • Task • Medium • Idea KAN-51 Analytics: PostHog               
  instrumentation                                                                                                     
  (FE + BE)  • Task • Medium • Idea
